title: request_bugfix: pin parallel per-step shop-msg read CLI naming in lead-po/bc-implementer/bc-reviewer subsections (round 3 of §4.4 closure on lead-bwu)
description:
Round 3 §4.4 closure for brief 001 items B+D. Parent chain: lead-231 (brief 001) -> lead-bwu (initial 9 scenarios) -> lead-e4g (scenario 46, lead-architect parallel) -> THIS BEAD.

Trigger: lead-e4g clarify reported asymmetry: Implementer extended fix to all four canonical templates (lead-po, lead-architect, bc-implementer, bc-reviewer) but scenario coverage only pinned lead-architect (scenario 46). The three sister subsections need parallel per-step pinning.

Carries scenarios 47, 48, 49 (one each for lead-po H3 'Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI', bc-implementer H2 'Your job', bc-reviewer H2 'What you read'). All three mirror scenario 46's three-Then structure (positive per-step CLI naming + bare-action-verbs regression + path-shape regression).

PO authored, Architect dispatched via shop-msg send request_bugfix. Implementer's terminal work_done on this bead closes lead-bwu and lead-e4g together.

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: scenario 46 closes lead-bwu clarify (per-step shop-msg read outbox in lead-architect 'Responding to BC clarify' subsection)
description:
Closure dispatch for the §4.4 clarify loop opened by lead-bwu.

Parent: lead-bwu (request_bugfix carrying 9 scenarios for brief lead-231 items B+D). Grandparent: lead-231 (brief 001).

Trigger: lead-bwu's clarify (BC Implementer/Reviewer in shopsystem-templates) flagged an asymmetry between lead-architect.md line 272 (bare 'Read the clarify' opening step in 'Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' subsection — no shop-msg read outbox cite on the same step) and lead-po.md lines 160-162 (explicit 'Read the BC's clarify via shop-msg read outbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>' form). Scenario 56b3ef5f48342b9b (= features/templates/20) pins template-wide invariants but does not pin per-step CLI naming inside procedural subsections.

Router scope ruling: in scope of brief lead-231 items B+D. Invariant 1 of brief 001 ('shop-msg is sole inter-shop messaging surface; consumers never touch storage directly') is per-step discipline; a Constraints clause is too weak to prevent regression.

Vehicle: request_bugfix. Discriminator:
- Q1 (capability exists?): YES. Empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-architect.md line 272 and lead-po.md lines 160-162; the asymmetry exists exactly as the clarify describes. Lead-architect 'Sending a message' subsection lines 261-266 already names shop-msg read inbox explicitly, demonstrating the BC template's authors knew the explicit form — the omission in the Responding subsection is a gap, not a pattern.
- Q2 (pinned?): NO at the procedural-step granularity inside the Responding subsection.
- Q3 (behavioral or flat?): behavioral — tightens existing unpinned discipline. Behavioral → request_bugfix.

Carries: features/templates/46-lead-architect-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin (PO-authored; tightened beyond the BC's draft to add a third Then condition pinning that path-shape references like 'outbox/', '<BC root>/outbox/', 'the outbox file' also cannot appear without naming the CLI on the same step).

§4.4 closure signaling: when shopsystem-templates emits work_done on THIS work_id, that work_done implicitly sign-offs the original 9 scenarios dispatched under lead-bwu (per shop-system §4.4 loop closure shape). The BC should NOT emit a separate work_done on lead-bwu retroactively. The dispatch description names this expectation.

Implementer breadth expectation: scenario 46 pins the lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify' subsection, but the underlying invariant (per-step shop-msg read outbox naming wherever a procedural step reads outbox storage) is template-wide. Implementer should scan all four canonical templates for the asymmetry pattern, not just edit the cited subsection.

title: Brief: shop bootstrap CLI surface (lead-shop + BC-shop scaffold over existing repo)
description: Brief 002 (in authoring): commits intent for a shop-bootstrap CLI surface — the user-facing way to take an existing repo and add the canonical shop scaffold (lead or BC). Building block for a later fully-automated product-setup workflow. Authoring driven by user-driver observation 2026-05-12: manual scaffolding does not scale across 5+ BC shops + ecommerce product staging.
notes:
2026-05-12 (Architect pre-state for dispatch): HELD pending lead-bwu work_done.

Pre-state verification:
- `shop-templates --help` confirms current CLI = {list, show}. No init/bootstrap/update subcommand → Q1 (capability exists?) is NO. Discriminator selects `assign_scenarios` for all 20 scenarios.
- `src/shop_templates/templates/` contains exactly 4 files: bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md, lead-architect.md, lead-po.md. No CLAUDE.md primer template exists yet → E's elevation is genuinely net-new package data.
- BC inbox state (via `shop-msg pending inbox --bc-root .../shopsystem-templates`): `lead-bwu request_bugfix` is pending (lead-bwu has been dispatched and is in_progress on the lead side but the BC has NOT yet picked it up — sat in inbox unprocessed at verification time).
- BC concurrency posture: bc-implementer template explicitly says "There is exactly one file there" (line 49). Template presumes singleton inbox. Depositing a second message while lead-bwu is in flight violates that template invariant.

Hold rationale:
- Brief 002 scope item E elevates the CURRENT content of /workspaces/shopsystem-product/CLAUDE.md and repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md into canonical templates. Both files are precisely what lead-bwu's scenarios 21 + 22 rewrite (shop-msg-CLI mailbox access; bd-decoupling).
- If brief 002 is dispatched before lead-bwu lands, item E captures pre-rewrite content (carrying direct-mailbox-inspection language and bd-as-precondition references). Subsequent lead-bwu rewrites the two source files but does NOT cover the newly-created canonical primer templates (their work_id scope is scoped to existing surfaces). Result: stale canonical primers shipping from package data, untracked.
- PO stance item 4 commits "Elevation and bootstrap-consumption ship in ONE dispatch." Items B+D depend on E's canonical CLAUDE.md package data existing; splitting (b) is not coherent.
- Option (a) plus a sequencing note in the dispatch description is advisory not enforced; the BC router doesn't serialize by intent-comment.

Decision: option (c). Wait for lead-bwu work_done to land; verify the two CLAUDE.md source files in their post-rewrite shape; then dispatch all 20 scenarios of brief 002 as one assign_scenarios.

Child bead for the eventual dispatch: NOT opened yet (per role contract; opens at dispatch time, non-dotted ID).

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: B+D (CLI-only mailbox access + bd-decoupling) for brief lead-231
description:
Dispatch of items B and D from brief lead-231 (briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md) to the shopsystem-templates BC as one combined request_bugfix carrying all 9 PO-authored scenarios (features/templates/17..25-*.gherkin).

Parent intent: lead-231 (brief). Items A, C, E of the brief are complete; B+D close it.

Vehicle: request_bugfix per Architect discriminator. Pre-state empirically verified:
- shop-msg --help confirms pending {inbox,outbox} and read {inbox,outbox} are live top-level subcommands.
- shop-msg respond mechanism_observation --help confirms --provenance-ref is live; --bd-ref is gone.
- Canonical templates today direct readers to direct mailbox inspection (bc-implementer.md lines 10, 17, 24, 118-119 reference <BC root>/inbox/ and <BC root>/outbox/; bc-reviewer.md line 17 same; lead-po.md line 161 carries the explicit 'read the file directly during a dispatch' punt; lead-architect.md line 233 carries 'Inbox filename convention: <work_id>.yaml').
- bc-implementer.md lines 176-196 instruct 'bd create' as numbered step 1 before shop-msg respond mechanism_observation, with --bd-ref in step 2 (both removed by current CLI).
- shopsystem-templates CLAUDE.md 'BC inbox / outbox protocol' section names <work_id>.yaml and <work_id>-<response_type>.yaml filename conventions and the set-difference-on-filenames characterization of unprocessed.
- Behavior unpinned: none of features/templates/01..16 cover CLI-only or bd-decoupling discipline; this is finding-5 territory (behavior exists, no scenario pins it) → request_bugfix.

Bundling: combined (a) chosen over split (b) because both items rewrite the same six surfaces; in particular the D-scope 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section lives inside bc-implementer.md whose lines 10/17/24/118-119 are B-scope. Splitting would force two consecutive dispatches over the same file and risk the second undoing the first.

Sequencing: lead-kq0 is CLOSED (verified via bd show lead-kq0), satisfying the brief's sequencing constraint that B+D wait on the lead-kq0 (PDR-001) role-complete restructure landing first.
notes: 2026-05-12 (Architect subagent) — §4.4 clarify loop opened by this dispatch is being closed via child bead lead-e4g (request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates carrying scenario 46). Per the dispatch description on lead-e4g, the BC's work_done on lead-e4g implicitly closes the original 9 scenarios dispatched here as well — the BC will NOT emit a separate work_done on lead-bwu. Keep lead-bwu in_progress until lead-e4g lands work_done; at that point close both together. shop-msg send exit 0; inbox scenario hash e6bdf2f33bfae0d1.

title: Dispatch fix for lead-231.2 blocker: replace 4 fabricated @scenario_hash tags in shopsystem-messaging mechanism_observation features with canonical values
description: Lead-side response to lead-231.2 work_done(blocked) under brief lead-231. Substantive bd-decoupling work in lead-231.2 landed correctly (6 scenarios pass, MechanismObservation bd_ref renamed to optional provenance_ref). Reviewer blocked because the Implementer's side-effect claim of 'recomputed' @scenario_hash tags on 4 mechanism_observation feature files in the BC's own repo carried fabricated values. Lead empirically reverified by piping each file through 'scenarios hash': Reviewer's canonicals match (950f7c76478e81c9 / 7c7a6d58fa71a60c / 00f34c55c7ea9b93 / d2d860f1c41d98e9), Implementer's claimed values are present in files and wrong. Dispatching request_maintenance (flat value-only fix, fully specified by Reviewer; no new scenarios). Non-dotted ID per lead-4wy. Parent (informally): lead-231. Supersedes the blocked branch of lead-231.2.
close_reason: complete: BC work_done verified end-to-end. Reread BC outbox via 'shop-msg read outbox --bc-root .../shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-5cv' — status complete, scenario_hashes:[] (correct: request_maintenance carries none). Spot-checked all 4 mechanism_observation feature files; each @scenario_hash tag equals 'cat file | scenarios hash' on the current body. Canonicals confirmed: respond_mechanism_observation.feature=950f7c76478e81c9; _collision=7c7a6d58fa71a60c; _path_separator=00f34c55c7ea9b93; _short_body=d2d860f1c41d98e9. Closes brief lead-231 item C (lead-231.2 substantive + lead-5cv tag fix) end-to-end.

title: Dispatch fix for lead-231.2 blocker: replace 4 fabricated @scenario_hash tags in shopsystem-messaging mechanism_observation features with canonical values
description: Lead-side response to lead-231.2 work_done(blocked). The substantive bd-decoupling work in lead-231.2 landed correctly (6 scenarios pass, MechanismObservation bd_ref renamed to optional provenance_ref). Reviewer blocked because the Implementer's side-effect claim of 'recomputed' @scenario_hash tags on 4 mechanism_observation feature files in the BC's own repo carried fabricated values. Lead empirically reverified by piping each file through 'scenarios hash': Reviewer's canonicals match (950f7c76478e81c9 / 7c7a6d58fa71a60c / 00f34c55c7ea9b93 / d2d860f1c41d98e9), Implementer's claimed values are present in files and wrong. Dispatching request_maintenance (flat value-only fix, fully specified by Reviewer; no new scenarios). lead-231.2 closed pointing here as resolution path.
close_reason: Recreating without parent to get non-dotted ID (lead-4wy: Clarify.work_id pattern rejects dots, so dotted work_ids are dispatch-fragile)

title: Dispatch item C of brief lead-231: catalog schema bd-decoupling (6 message types) to shopsystem-messaging
description: request_bugfix dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item C of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). Bundles 6 scenarios from features/messaging/08..13-*.gherkin pinning the invariant 'minimal valid <type> can be constructed with no required bd-related field' for each of the 6 currently-implemented catalog schemas: AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, RequestMaintenance, Clarify, WorkDone, MechanismObservation. Pre-state empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/schemas.py: 5 of 6 schemas (RequestMaintenance, AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, Clarify, WorkDone) define NO bd_* field at all — their scenarios are regression pins against unpinned existing behavior. MechanismObservation.bd_ref is required (Field pattern '^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*-[a-z0-9]+$', line 178); its scenario tightens by demanding bd_ref become optional / removed / renamed to provenance_ref (Implementer's pick consistent with invariant). request_scenario_register and request_shop_card are deferred per the brief — no schemas exist for them in catalog/schemas.py. Bundled (not split) because the 6 scenarios share one invariant; the Implementer needs to see surface coherence. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).
close_reason: work_done(blocked) received. 6 assigned scenarios pass and substantive bd-decoupling work landed correctly (MechanismObservation.bd_ref -> optional provenance_ref). Reviewer terminated this loop on a concrete side-effect defect: 4 @scenario_hash tags in BC's own features/respond_mechanism_observation*.feature files carry fabricated values. Lead reverified empirically (cat file | scenarios hash matches Reviewer's canonicals; Implementer's claimed values present in files and wrong). Resolution path is a new fix-loop dispatched as lead-5cv (request_maintenance, value-only flat fix). This work_id is closed because the Reviewer terminated the loop — re-opening here would conflict with the §4.4 sealed-decision posture. lead-5cv carries live work going forward.

title: Dispatch item A of brief lead-231: shop-msg CLI surface (pending enumeration + read inbox + --bc filter) to shopsystem-messaging
description: assign_scenarios dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item A of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). Carries 7 scenarios from features/messaging/01..07-*.gherkin pinning: list-pending enumeration (empty + populated + processed-exclusion + lead-side --bc filter) and read inbox (happy path + missing work_id + schema-invalid). Pre-state empirically verified: shop-msg today exposes only respond/send/read with read having only outbox subcommand; no pending/list/next subcommand exists; no --bc filter exists. New capability => assign_scenarios. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).
close_reason: work_done(complete) received. 7 scenario hashes match dispatch one-to-one (2f028ee351fc512c, dc27ddad94f50479, 2e585da377f68d5c, d71fb7f28c4cd561, 350af5ec19e38974, 6b191cfc691ede7f, 92021082218ffa87). Post-state spot-check: 'shop-msg --help' now lists pending+read subcommands; 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead-root <root> --bc shopsystem-messaging' returns structured listing of both work_done responses (exit=0); 'shop-msg read inbox --bc-root <bc> --work-id lead-5cv' works (used to verify follow-up dispatch). CLI surface change landed substantively. No scenario register exists for shopsystem-messaging yet (lead-otu).

title: Brief: inter-shop messaging encapsulation (shop-msg as sole surface; self-contained messages)
description: Lead-shop brief authored from mechanism_observation 'shopsystem-templates-kin' against lead-kq0. Names two invariants (shop-msg is the sole inter-shop messaging surface; inter-shop messages are self-contained), the bd-decoupling corollary, and five scope items (A: shop-msg CLI surface; B: templates rewrite; C: catalog schema decoupling from bd; D: template-language decoupling from bd; E: spec edits to sections 4 and 5). Sequencing: A+C+E parallel; B+D wait on lead-kq0 (PDR-001) closing. Scenario authoring deferred until Architect verifies BC pre-state and picks vehicles. Artifact: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md.
notes:
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, brief 001 items B+D, round 3 follow-up) — BC clarify on lead-e4g (request_bugfix carrying scenario 46) confirmed the regression vulnerability is present in three sister subsections that scenario 46 does not pin: lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' (line 158), bc-implementer '## Your job' (line 26), bc-reviewer '## What you read' (line 15). The Implementer's textual FIX extended across all four templates correctly, but coverage only pinned one. Without parallel scenarios, future edits to the other three subsections could regress to bare-verb 'Read the inbox YAML' (or similar) and only the template-wide encapsulation scenarios (6adb97ba0d0498c5, ff97230bf1e36a81, f279d66b8313e3fc) would gate them — and the BC's clarify text correctly notes those are too weak for per-step discipline (a bare 'Read' step coexists with 'shop-msg read inbox' elsewhere in the same template, satisfying template-wide substring presence while leaving the procedural step ambiguous).

Scope decision (router): option (a) accepted — three parallel scenarios mirroring 46's shape. Not (b) (BC's textual fix needs scenario coverage to be load-bearing). Not (c) (tightening the template-wide encapsulation scenarios with per-step granularity would lose the presence/absence checks they currently make; per-subsection scenarios are the cleaner shape). Outline-form consolidation deferred — scenario 46 is already dispatched + hashed; a brief revision can fold all four into a Scenario Outline if the pattern continues to recur.

Authored: features/templates/47-lead-po-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/48-bc-implementer-your-job-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin; features/templates/49-bc-reviewer-what-you-read-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-inbox-per-step.gherkin. Each mirrors scenario 46 exactly: locate the subsection by heading boundary (## or ### until next heading), three Then conditions — (i) every step whose action is reading the mailbox names the literal CLI substring on the same step, (ii) no bare-verb regression mode, (iii) no path-shape regression mode. Vary only by template name, subsection heading text, and target substring (read outbox vs. read inbox).

Pre-state verification of BC's sketch: subsection headings as sketched (verified by grep). Two asymmetries noted but kept out of scope of this dispatch:
- bc-implementer 'Your job' step 1 (line 28) names BOTH 'shop-msg read inbox' (reading) AND 'shop-msg pending inbox' (listing). The new scenario 48 pins only the read-inbox action, parallel to scenario 46's narrow target. The pending-inbox-listing action is already covered template-wide by scenarios 17/18 (uses-shop-msg-cli-for-mailbox-access). Same shape applies to bc-reviewer 'What you read' step 1 (lines 17-20) — scenario 49 pins only read-inbox.
- bc-implementer 'Your job' is H2 (## Your job); lead-po's target is H3 (### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI); bc-reviewer's target is H2 (## What you read); lead-architect's target (scenario 46) is H3. Scenario 46's locator clause 'ends at the next heading of depth two (##) or depth three (###)' handles both depths uniformly; new scenarios reuse the same clause verbatim. Consistent.

Round-4 outlook (flag): With round 3 closing the four-template per-subsection-CLI-naming regression mode, the remaining brief-001 B+D regression-mode surface is:
- Procedural CLI naming for the OTHER mailbox actions inside the same/adjacent procedural subsections. Specifically: writing the outbox response. lead-architect '### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' step 4 cites 'shop-msg respond clarify'; lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4 cites the same. But no scenario currently pins per-step CLI naming for the RESPOND action. Same regression mode: a future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only the template-wide scenarios would gate it. If round 4 is desired, the same Scenario-Outline-amenable shape applies across all four templates' write-outbox / write-inbox actions.
- Procedural CLI naming for 'shop-msg respond work_done' (Implementer/Reviewer) and 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' (Implementer). bc-implementer 'Your job' step 4 names 'shop-msg respond work_done'; 'Surfacing mechanism observations' section names 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation'; bc-reviewer 'Outcomes' names both 'shop-msg respond work_done' and 'shop-msg respond clarify'. None of these are pinned by per-step scenarios.

Recommendation: defer round 4 to a deliberate brief revision rather than chasing it dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline — and the cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly. Until then, the four parallel scenarios are correct and load-bearing.

Did NOT dispatch. Did NOT compose shop-msg send. Architect opens a request_bugfix dispatch bead and sends the three new scenarios as additive to lead-e4g's coverage. (Open question for Architect: dispatch as a separate work_id, or amend lead-e4g? The BC's clarify is open against lead-e4g; the cleanest closure is one new request_bugfix work_id whose description references lead-e4g's clarify as the trigger.)

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs (lead-231.1, lead-231.2) as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots.

This is a latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on either lead-231.1 or lead-231.2, the BC-side 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' will fail schema validation. The protocol's symmetry across message types breaks at clarify-time.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas. Three shapes worth considering — (a) tighten AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix/RequestMaintenance/WorkDone/MechanismObservation to share Clarify's pattern, constraining work_id naming uniformly; (b) loosen Clarify to match the others (just str), accepting more chaos but unblocking; (c) pick a shared canonical pattern (probably (a)'s shape with an explicit grammar) and apply uniformly with a single Pydantic type alias. Architect's call after pre-state verification.

Adjacent finding (do not scope here, just note): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches, and the encapsulation invariant from lead-231 prohibits rm-ing inbox files. The two existing lead-231.1/.2 dispatches will tolerate this gap because they're more likely to round-trip via work_done than clarify, but recall-side encapsulation is a structural concern worth its own future brief.

Related: lead-231 (brief that surfaced this).

title: Restructure lead-po and lead-architect templates to be role-complete (PDR-001)
description:
Lead-shop work item tracking the dispatch to shopsystem-templates BC for the
role-complete restructure of lead-po.md and lead-architect.md.

Intent driver: PDR-001 (pdr/001-role-templates-role-complete.md).

The PO subagent authors scenarios in this repo's features/templates/ pinning
what role-completeness means against §3.2's activity catalogue. The Architect
subagent then verifies the templates BC's pre-state empirically, applies the
message-type discriminator, and dispatches via shop-msg send to the
shopsystem-templates BC.

This beads ID is the work_id that flows outward in the inter-shop message
per §6.
notes:
Architect dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates BC on 2026-05-12.

Empirical pre-state verification: ran `shop-templates show lead-po` and
`shop-templates show lead-architect`. Confirmed: neither template names the

title: bc-implementer singleton-inbox presumption constrains lead-shop dispatch cadence
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during brief-002 Architect pre-state verification.

The bc-implementer canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md line 49 says verbatim 'There is exactly one file there.' The Implementer subagent's role contract presumes a singleton inbox. The bc-router CLAUDE.md does not enforce serialization either — its dispatch rule is 'any unprocessed inbox dispatches the Implementer' with no ordering guarantee.

Concrete consequence: the lead shop cannot dispatch a new message to a BC while another work_id is still in flight (still in the BC's inbox, not yet processed to outbox). Architect for brief-002 detected this and held dispatch because lead-bwu was still pending in the templates BC inbox.

Design question: should the protocol/template
- (a) explicitly enforce serial in-flight (one message at a time per BC; lead shop blocks on outbox before next send), formalizing the current presumption?
- (b) relax the singleton presumption (Implementer template handles N-message inbox; BC-router picks one to dispatch per turn)?
- (c) introduce a queue concept in shop-msg (lead can deposit multiple; BC processes in FIFO order; protocol exposes 'next' subcommand)?

(a) is the lowest-touch and matches current template behavior. (c) is the most flexible and may align with the brief-001 invariant (shop-msg encapsulates storage). (b) is a middle ground.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done(blocked) protocol gap) — same shape of latent protocol constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Currently lead-bwu remains pending in templates BC inbox; brief-002 dispatch is held until it lands. This bead captures the structural concern, not the operational hold.

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.
